canonicalEncoding: UTF-8 without BOM, LF, exact file bytes

repoPath.framing: magic[8] || version_u16 || component_count_u32 || component[component_count]; no terminator and no trailing bytes
repoPath.emptyPath: component_count zero is the canonical repository-relative empty path

kinds.name: PortableUtf8
kinds.canonicalUse: required when the native component is representable by a portable scalar string

kinds.name: UnixBytes
kinds.canonicalUse: required only when PortableUtf8 cannot represent the component or when a Unix-valid backslash byte makes it non-portable

kinds.name: WindowsWtf16
kinds.payload: exact unsigned UTF-16 code units, each encoded u16 big-endian
kinds.canonicalUse: required only when at least one unpaired surrogate prevents PortableUtf8 representation

commonRejections: embedded backslash in PortableUtf8 or WindowsWtf16
decoderCanonicality: reject a payload length beyond the remaining bytes
reject malformed, overlong, surrogate-containing, or non-shortest PortableUtf8
reject UnixBytes when the same native component has a canonical PortableUtf8 encoding
reject WindowsWtf16 when all code units form Unicode scalars and therefore have a canonical PortableUtf8 encoding
reject separators, NUL, dot, and dot-dot before identity creation

repoPath.ordering: unsigned lexicographic order over the complete canonical repo-path.v1 byte string

repositoryRoot.framing: magic[8] || version_u16 || platform_u8 || address_kind_u8 || address_prefix || component_count_u32 || components || physical_identity

addressKinds.name: WindowsDrive
addressKinds.prefix: one uppercase ASCII byte A through Z

addressKinds.name: WindowsUNC
addressKinds.prefix: two canonical component records for server and share

addressKinds.name: WindowsVolumeGuid
addressKinds.prefix: 16 GUID bytes in RFC 4122/network textual order

windowsPrefixRules: extended drive and UNC prefixes lower to WindowsDrive or WindowsUNC
a volume GUID path lowers to WindowsVolumeGuid
device namespace roots such as \\.\, \\?\GLOBALROOT, and native \Device\ roots are rejected

repoPathDto.canonicalBase64: RFC 4648 standard alphabet with required padding and no whitespace; decode then canonical re-encode must equal the input
repoPathDto.utf8: optional slash-joined Unicode-scalar string only when every component is PortableUtf8; it must re-encode to canonicalBase64

wireAndNativeIO.paths0Unix: NUL-delimited slash-separated exact native bytes; NUL is never legal inside one path
wireAndNativeIO.paths0Windows: NUL-delimited slash-separated canonical WTF-8; paired surrogates encode as one scalar UTF-8 sequence, unpaired surrogates encode as three-byte WTF-8, and CESU-8 for a valid pair is rejected
wireAndNativeIO.matchBytes: slash-separated PortableUtf8 payload, UnixBytes payload, or canonical Windows WTF-8 payload; no Unicode normalization

goldenVectors.meaning: one WindowsWtf16 component with code units D800 0061
Windows C:/repo with volume serial 1 and FILE_ID_128 00..0f
Windows //server/share/repo with volume serial 1 and FILE_ID_128 00..0f
Windows volume 00112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccddeeff /repo with volume serial 1 and FILE_ID_128 00..0f

rejectionVectors: PortableUtf8 containing slash, backslash, or NUL
WindowsWtf16 with an odd payload length
WindowsWtf16 containing only valid scalar sequences
valid value followed by trailing bytes

compiledContract.artifactIdentity: SHA-256 of exact checked-in file bytes, supplied by the candidate manifest and compiled into AnalysisContractId
compiledContract.runtimeForm: one lumin-model project-owned pure codec generated from this artifact; lumin-inventory supplies native atoms and lumin-protocol supplies wire/display adapters by calling that codec, with no runtime artifact read or duplicate encoder
compiledContract.requiredCheck: architecture-check compares artifact bytes, the single generated model-codec digest, all golden/rejection vectors, canonical decoder behavior, and owner-specific native/wire adapter boundaries
